div1.entryFree: Cleanly, adv., 1) in a clean manner, without stain: and live c. as a nobleman should do, H4A V, 4, 169. -- 2) quite, entirely: ceasing their clamorous cry till they have singled with much ado the cold fault c. out, Ven. 694. hast not thou full often struck a doe, and borne her c. by the keeper's nose? Tit. II, 1, 94 (== in the very face of the keeper).
